subject: english
topic_title: Structure: Prefixes, Negation, Pluralization, and Tense Suffixes
source_title: Third Term SS1 English Language Lesson Notes
learning_objectives: Identify the prefixes listed in the note

sections.heading: Prefixes and Meanings
sections.content: The note lists prefixes such as extra-, ex-, inter-, trans-, post-, pre-, super-, sub-, ab-, ante-, and anti-, with examples like extraordinary, intermarry, transatlantic, postgraduate, prenatal, and antisocial.

sections.heading: Suffixes
sections.content: Negation suffixes include -less as in childless and useless. Pluralization suffixes include -ies and -es as in babies and boxes. Tense-related forms in the note include -en and -ze as in strengthen, brighten, and mobilize.
